title: Column for storing passwords/ sensitive information
excerpt: I would love the option to add a ‘password’ column that is used specifically for storing passwords and other sensitive information. Having the text covered by ‘**********’ until selected would help keep information priva…

posts.author: nicholasb (NIcholas Breaden)
posts.cookedHtml:
I would love the option to add a ‘password’ column that is used specifically for storing passwords and other sensitive information. Having the text covered by ‘**********’ until selected would help keep information private from people passing by a desk.

Ability to audit this column specifically would also be good, so we can see who is accessing/ changing passwords.

We want to use Monday to store some documentation, and this feature would allow us to do that!

posts.author: JohnW (John Waller)
posts.cookedHtml:
Have you explored column permissions in Monday?

Support

Board Permissions

3 min read Feature   We offer several different types of permissions on monday.com—account permissions, workspace permissions, board types, board permissions, and column permissions. In this a...

You can restrict column view to specific people.

  • Doesn’t solve the problem of people seeing the information on your screen as board owner as they pass by your desk
  • There’s no option to obfuscate the password that I’m aware of.

In the board Activity Log you can see who changed the password and when.

posts.author: vilmondes.queiroz (Vilmondes Queiroz)
posts.cookedHtml: Although we can set up column permissions, it would be nice if we could have a password type column to obfuscate the password.

posts.author: nicholasb (NIcholas Breaden)
posts.cookedHtml: We are still wanting this column type, as it would allow us to use Monday.com and do away with our password documentation platform

posts.author: zensystems (AJ Howard-Dobson)
posts.cookedHtml:
A password/sensitive info column would allow us to move all of our documentation into Monday - an exciting prospect as it would reduce our costs and bring everything into one place.

The data stored in the password column would need to be encrypted at rest to pass security requirements.

If this feature were added, Monday would be able to compete with IT document management systems and attract even more customers.

posts.author: joshsummit (Joshua McIntyre)
posts.cookedHtml: A data masking/visibility feature like this would be very helpful! A basic application of this is for SSN and other sensitive data information. Please continue to develop for this!

posts.author: ChristopherR2D2 (Christopher Ream)
posts.cookedHtml: We, too, would appreciate this type of data format for sensitive data. Thanks!

posts.author: anon29275264 (anon29275264)
posts.cookedHtml:
I’m going to lend my support to this as well.

Sometimes users want to HIDE a certain value unless a user clicks on it to reveal it (then have it hide again after a period of time.)

This is useful for Last-4 of SSNs etc. that may need to be accessed in certain scenarios but where you still don’t want that visible except when actually needed.

posts.author: idodekov (Iliyan Dodekov (Deview Studios))
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi Folks, I’m happy to share there’s a new app Passwords & Secrets Vault, that allows you to store secrets and optionally share them with your team.

Key Features

Secure Storage of Various Secrets: Safeguard any type of confidential information directly within your monday.com workspaces. From passwords to private notes, keep everything secure in one place.

Seamless Team Collaboration: Authorize team members to specific vaults, enabling smooth and secure collaboration.

Team Secrets Management: Store and manage secrets for various tools and platforms, ensuring that team members have secure access without the need for insecure sharing methods.

Confidential Project Details: Keep sensitive project information, like financial data or strategic plans, secure and accessible only to those who need it.
